snippets: The Amberg-Sulzbach region offers various day trip destinations.
Day trips from Amberg include culinary, nature, and cultural experiences.
Regional culinary specialties are available in the Amberg-Sulzbach district.
The Riesenwindbeutel is a large cream puff specialty from Erlheim.
The Schlachtschüssel is a traditional Bavarian dish.
The Kastler Schneeball is a regional pastry specialty.
Café Hammermühle is located approximately 23 kilometers from Amberg.
Café Hammermühle is situated in the Lauterach valley.
Café Hammermühle offers organic food.
Café Hammermühle uses regional ingredients.
Café Hammermühle focuses on sustainable agriculture.
Café Anni is located in Schwend.
Café Anni is approximately 20 kilometers from Amberg.
Café Anni is a family-run guesthouse.
Café Anni serves traditional Bavarian cuisine.
Café Anni offers homemade cakes and tarts.
Café Anni is located on a southern slope.
Naturhof Landfreude is located in Hainstetten.
Naturhof Landfreude is approximately 18 kilometers from Amberg.
Hainstetten is the highest village in the district.
Naturhof Landfreude includes Café Oma Gerti.
Café Oma Gerti opened in July 2022.
Café Oma Gerti offers homemade cakes.
Café Oma Gerti offers a breakfast menu.
Café Oma Gerti has a terrace.
The terrace is open in summer and winter.
The location offers views of surrounding hills.
On clear days, the Arber mountain is visible.
The café sells decorative and household items.
Lauterach trout is a regional fish specialty.
Lauterach trout is available in local guesthouses.
The trout is considered a delicacy for fish lovers.
Illschwang offers experiences related to hop cultivation.
Visitors can explore hop fields in Illschwang.
Illschwang has a Hop Museum.
Illschwang includes a simultaneous church.
The simultaneous church is used by both Catholics and Protestants.
Beer tasting is available in Illschwang.
Visitors can learn about hop harvesting.
Day trips include outdoor activities in the surrounding region.
The Ochsentour is a hiking route in Auerbach.
The Ochsentour has a length of approximately 2.6 kilometers.
The Ochsentour passes through a nature reserve.
The Ochsentour includes sightings of wild cattle.
The Ossingerturm can be visited as a day trip.
The Ossingerturm offers panoramic views of the region.
Monte Kaolino is a day trip destination for leisure activities.
Monte Kaolino includes sand skiing and outdoor recreation.
The Kartmotodrom Amberg can be visited for kart racing.
Haunritz is a destination for hikers and climbers.
Haunritz is located in the Högenbachtal.
Haunritz features distinctive rock formations.
Unique experiences are available in the Amberg-Sulzbach region.
The Landschaftskino Ensdorf is a panoramic viewpoint.
The Landschaftskino offers wide views of the surrounding landscape.
The Landschaftskino can be accessed via a stairway.
The Landschaftskino can be accessed via a Way of the Cross path.
The Schulmuseum is a historical school museum.
The Schulmuseum includes classrooms from around 1875.
The Schulmuseum includes classrooms from around 1920.
The Schulmuseum is suitable for families and school groups.
The Osterhöhle is a cave open to visitors.
The Osterhöhle is open on weekends during summer.
The Gießkannenbaum is a tree decorated with watering cans.
The Gießkannenbaum features approximately 250 watering cans.
The installation is located at the entrance to Schmidmühlen.
The installation is displayed from Easter to All Saints’ Day.
The Asphaltkapelle is a chapel made entirely of asphalt.
The Asphaltkapelle was built in 2002.
The Asphaltkapelle is located near Ensdorf.
The chapel is open year-round.
Family-friendly day trips are available around Amberg.
The Freudenberger Märchenweg is a fairy tale trail.
The Märchenweg has a length of approximately 1.7 kilometers.
The trail passes through forest and meadow landscapes.
The trail includes wooden storytelling stations.
Stories include traditional fairy tales.
The trail requires sturdy footwear.
The trail includes a viewpoint called Böhmerwaldblick.
The viewpoint offers distant mountain views.
The Fledermaushaus Hohenburg focuses on bat conservation.
The facility educates visitors about endangered species.
The Eseltour Hackern offers guided donkey tours.
Donkey tours allow relaxed exploration of nature.
The Wildschweingehege Waldhaus allows observation of wild boars.
The site includes a playground for children.
